mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 65 percent of triggered instances, invalidating the negative predictive signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital spending growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative median forward returns over 60 trading days in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher rates, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day returns in more than 70 percent of tightening period observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend pace expands corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth signals show zero predictive power with Rank IC equal to zero over 5-year out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above threshold consistently precede positive 20-day market returns in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment indicates robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over 20-day horizons in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a late-cycle expansion signals weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, producing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Housing start declines correlate with positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of late-cycle observations.
